title: Fix: rust-player never called init_assets, so prompts silently failed
description: RealtimeRunnerBridge::set_prompt() had no effect because tokenizer_/text_encoder_interpreter_/quantizer_interpreter_ were always null -- init_assets()/load_musiccoca_model() was never called from the Rust bridge. fetch_musiccoca_tokens() silently set text_encoder_status_=3 (error) every time, so musiccoca_tokens_ stayed on its hardcoded kDefaultMusicCoCaTokensPiano value forever, regardless of blend weights or call ordering. Fixed by: (1) adding init_assets FFI wrapper, (2) calling it with the --resources/config resources path before set_prompt, (3) polling get_quantizer_status() until encode completes (status==2) before unmuting audio. Verified via [MagentaRT] Combined Prompt tokens log line showing real non-default RVQ tokens.
close_reason: Fixed by wiring init_assets() into rust-player before set_prompt/load_model; verified via [MagentaRT] Combined Prompt tokens log showing real non-default RVQ tokens per prompt. Documented in docs/realtime-audio.md and rust-player/README.md.

title: Add Pause/Resume to swift-player (bypass without stopping inference)
description: The current Play/Stop button loses the audio context on every stop (trigger_reset on play). Add a distinct Pause state using set_bypass(true/false) that silences output while keeping the inference thread running and the context window intact. State machine: Not Loaded → (load) → Stopped → (play) → Playing → (pause) → Paused → (resume) → Playing, or (stop) → Stopped from either Playing or Paused. UI: while Playing show both a Pause button and the existing Stop button; while Paused show Resume (green, same position as Play) and Stop. Resume calls only magentart_set_bypass(ref, false) — no trigger_reset, no start, no priming delay. Add isPaused: Bool to PlayerState. Update menu bar Space shortcut to toggle Play/Pause rather than Play/Stop. Stop remains a separate destructive action (clears context).

title: Add mid-playback Reset Context button to swift-player
description: trigger_reset() clears the audio context window and re-anchors generation to the text prompt without stopping the inference thread. The transition is click-free (one-frame fade envelope). Expose as a 'Reset' button (systemImage: arrow.counterclockwise) in PlayerView, visible only while playing. Most useful paired with a prompt change: user edits prompt, hits Reset, model immediately starts fresh from the new style. Add magentart_trigger_reset is already in the bridge. Just needs a button in PlayerView calling playerManager.resetContext() which calls magentart_trigger_reset(ref). See docs/mrt2-prompt-and-drift.md.

title: Expose cfg_musiccoca as Prompt Strength slider in swift-player
description: cfg_musiccoca is the primary dial between prompt adherence and free generation. Currently defined in ParameterValues (cfgmusiccoca: Float = 3.0) but never forwarded to the engine. Add: (1) magentart_set_cfg_musiccoca to the C bridge header and .mm implementation calling set_cfg_musiccoca(). (2) Call it in applyParameters(). (3) Add a Slider in PlayerView ranging 1.0–8.0, labelled 'Prompt Strength' (not the raw param name), with value callout. Recommended default 3.0. See docs/mrt2-prompt-and-drift.md for CFG semantics.

title: Expose text style prompt in swift-player UI
description: The text prompt is the primary creative control in MRT2 — it steers MusicCoCa to embed a style direction ('jazz piano', 'ambient synth', 'heavy metal drums') into the generation. Currently: magentart_set_text_prompt() exists in the C bridge but is never called; ParameterValues has no textPrompt field; PlayerView has no text input. Changes required: (1) add textPrompt: String = '' to ParameterValues, (2) call magentart_set_text_prompt in applyParameters(), (3) add a TextField or editable label to PlayerView below the model name, styled as the primary input — submitting on Return and on play start. Consider showing placeholder text like 'e.g. jazz piano, heavy metal, ambient synth'. Prompt should be applied immediately on change while playing (the engine accepts live updates).

title: Swift Player C++ Engine Integration
description: Wire swift-player to the shared libmagentart-core.a from mrt2-build/. Covers the C bridge, Package.swift linkage, PlayerManager engine calls, real audio render, metrics polling, and MIDI routing. Depends on the Shared MRT2 Build System epic (3vy) being complete.

title: Update Package.swift to compile bridge and link libmagentart-core.a
description: Add a clangTarget 'MagentaBridge' in Package.swift that compiles swift-player/bridge/magentart_bridge.mm and exposes its header. Link against mrt2-build/libmagentart-core.a. The path to mrt2-build must be read from the MRT2_BUILD_DIR env var (set by the root Makefile export) with a fallback of ../mrt2-build. Use ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment in Package.swift to read it. Update the build-swift target in swift-player/Makefile to pass MRT2_BUILD_DIR explicitly via the environment so swift build picks it up. The executable target magenta-player must declare MagentaBridge as a dependency.

title: Write C bridge for RealtimeRunner (swift-player/bridge/)
description: Swift cannot call C++ directly. Create a thin C API over magentart::core::RealtimeRunner that Swift can import via a bridging header. Add swift-player/bridge/include/magentart_bridge.h (C header, no C++ types) and swift-player/bridge/magentart_bridge.mm (Objective-C++ implementation). API surface needed: magentart_create() -> opaque handle, magentart_destroy(handle), magentart_load_model(handle, path) -> bool, magentart_start(handle), magentart_stop(handle), magentart_set_bypass(handle, bool), magentart_trigger_reset(handle), magentart_set_temperature(handle, float), magentart_set_top_k(handle, uint32), magentart_set_text_prompt(handle, const char*, float weight), magentart_set_midi_gate(handle, bool), magentart_get_metrics(handle, out struct), magentart_read_audio(handle, float* left, float* right, uint32 frames) -> int. Base the implementation on rust-player/src/bridge.h which already maps these calls correctly.

title: Add Cargo.toml linking configuration to rust-player/
description: Update rust-player/Cargo.toml with cxx-build config to link against shared mrt2-build/ libmagentart-core.a

title: Rust Player Build Epic
description: Epic to create/build the complete Rust player build system including Makefile, Cargo.toml modifications, and C++ linking strategy

title: Rust Player Makefile
description: Create the rust-player/Makefile that provides targets for building against shared mrt2-build/ artifacts

title: Add root-level Makefile orchestrating mrt2-build, swift-player, rust-player
description: The monorepo root has no top-level Makefile. Add one with targets: setup, build-mrt2 (delegates to mrt2-build/Makefile), build-swift (delegates to swift-player/Makefile), build-rust (delegates to rust-player/), run-swift, all, clean. This is the primary developer entry point so each player doesn't need its own mrt2 setup step.

title: Add Makefile to rust-player/ for building against shared mrt2-build/
description: Create Makefile for rust-player/ that handles setup, builds against shared mrt2-build/ artifacts, and provides development commands

title: Create mrt2-build directory structure at project root
description: Create the shared mrt2-build/ directory at project root with build artifacts (static libraries, include paths) for both Swift and Rust player projects

title: Memory Banks: save and restore generation seeds in swift-player
description: Allow users to bookmark a moment of generation they like and return to it later. When the user hears something cool, a 'Save Memory' button should: (1) call start_recording if not already running, grab the last ~20s of PCM audio via get_recorded_audio() into a WAV file, (2) call save_state(path) to checkpoint the transformer KV cache to a .safetensors file, (3) snapshot current textPrompt + ParameterValues to a JSON file, (4) call get_waveform_peaks(200) for a thumbnail. All written to ~/Documents/Magenta/magenta-rt-v2/memories/<timestamp>/. A Memory Banks panel lists saved seeds showing: waveform thumbnail (drawn with Canvas), timestamp, prompt text excerpt. Loading a memory offers two actions: 'Continue' (load_state — exact continuation, same musical moment) and 'Re-seed' (prefill_state with WAV samples — fresh generation inspired by that audio). Bridge additions needed: magentart_save_state, magentart_load_state, magentart_prefill_state, magentart_start_recording, magentart_stop_recording, magentart_get_recorded_sample_count, magentart_get_recorded_audio, magentart_get_waveform_peaks. See realtime_runner.h — all APIs exist. See docs/mrt2-integration.md for prefill semantics.

title: Disable Play button and Space shortcut when no model is loaded
description: Currently pressing Play before loading a model calls togglePlay(), which hits the 'notLoaded' guard in PlayerManager and surfaces an errorAlert. Better UX: disable the Play button entirely when no model is loaded so the error never fires. Changes: (1) In PlayerView, pass a disabled state to PlayPauseButton based on !magentart_is_loaded(ref) — expose this as a computed property on PlayerManager (var isModelLoaded: Bool). (2) Apply .opacity(0.4) and .allowsHitTesting(false) or use SwiftUI .disabled() on the button when not loaded. (3) Also disable the Space keyboard shortcut in MagentaPlayerApp commands when not loaded — post the notification only if a model is loaded, or let PlayerManager silently no-op. (4) Show a subtitle hint under the button like 'Load a model to begin' when not loaded.

title: Expose cfg_notes and cfg_drums sliders in swift-player
description: cfg_notes and cfg_drums are CFG guidance weights for melodic note conditioning and rhythmic/drum conditioning respectively. Both defined in ParameterValues (cfgnotes: Float = 1.0, cfgdrums: Float = 1.0) but not forwarded to engine. Add magentart_set_cfg_notes and magentart_set_cfg_drums to the C bridge. Call in applyParameters(). Expose as compact sliders (range 0.0–4.0) in an expandable Advanced section or alongside the Prompt Strength slider. Label them 'Note Guidance' and 'Drum Guidance'. See docs/mrt2-prompt-and-drift.md.

title: Update documentation for XDG configuration and parameters
description: Update rust-player/README.md with details on ~/.config/magenta-rust-player/config.toml layout, how to view/adjust default parameters, and subcommand help guides

title: Implement play and config subcommands in Rust player CLI
description: Refactor the clap parser to support play (for real-time streaming) and config (for listing and adjusting default config settings) subcommands

title: Rust Player Client & DX
description: Epic covering all Rust-specific CLI client features, developer experience (DX), audio resampling, parameter tuning, and local configurations

title: Implement XDG config file support for Rust player parameters
description: Combine the directories crate with serde and toml to load default parameters (model path, resources, prompts, volumes) from ~/.config/magenta/config.toml, allowing CLI flags to override them

title: Add WAV recording option to Rust player CLI
description: Implement start_recording(), stop_recording(), and get_recorded_audio() FFI methods, and add a --record <WAV_PATH> flag to the Rust player CLI to record and save a 10s audio clip as a WAV file

title: Expand Rust player CLI with full set of MRT2 tuning parameters
description: Update clap parser with --cfg-text, --cfg-notes, --cfg-drums, --drumless, and --volume-db parameters, and de-conflict short flags (-m for model, -g for midi-gate, -t for temperature, -k for topk, -p for prompt)
close_reason: Added --cfg-text/--cfg-notes/--cfg-drums/--drumless/--volume-db flags + config keys (defaults matched to engine factory values: 3.0/5.0/1.0/false/0.0). Fixed pre-existing short-flag collisions (-t was temperature+topk, -m was model+midi-gate) -> -m/-r/-p/-t/-k/-g. Fixed clap negative-number parsing for volume_db via allow_hyphen_values. Verified via --help and live runs.

title: Add volume slider and mute toggle to swift-player UI
description: ParameterValues already has volume: Float = 0.8 (dB) and mute: Bool = false, and applyParameters() already calls magentart_set_volume_db and magentart_set_mute. Only the UI is missing. Add to PlayerView: (1) a Slider for volume in the range -60 to +6 dB, labeled with the current dB value, placed below the prompt field or in a controls row near Play/Stop. (2) A mute toggle button (speaker.slash.fill / speaker.fill icon) that immediately silences without stopping inference — use set_bypass is wrong here, magentart_set_mute is the right call. Both controls should call applyParameters() or dedicated setVolume/setMute methods on change. Volume should also be settable via keyboard shortcut if natural (Cmd+Up/Down or similar). Mute should be visually obvious when active (e.g. red tint or strikethrough on level meters).

title: Real-time rolling waveform visualizer in swift-player
description: Add a live stereo waveform display to PlayerView that shows ~1.5 seconds of rolling audio history, updating at 30 fps. Design: extend LevelTracker with a lock-free ring buffer (e.g. 4096 Float slots per channel). In the AVAudioSourceNode render block, after reading from magentart_read_audio_stereo, downsample each render burst to ~8 representative peak samples and write into the ring buffer. In PlayerView, add a WaveformView using TimelineView(.animation(minimumInterval: 1.0/30)) + Canvas. Canvas reads the ring buffer and draws two overlapping polylines (L=blue matching the level meter, R=green) on a dark background. The view should: fade in when playing starts, show a flat line at 0 when stopped, be ~120pt tall and span the full width of the window below the controls. No new C bridge calls needed — all data comes from the existing render block. Optional stretch: also bridge get_waveform_peaks() to show generation history as a separate waveform history strip below the rolling view.

title: Update rust-player README with Makefile usage and model test commands
description: Document how to use the rust-player/Makefile targets (make build, run, clean) and provide copy-pasteable CLI commands to test both mrt2_small and mrt2_base models

title: Add in-app model download sheet to swift-player
description: Currently users must run 'mrt models download <name>' in a terminal before using the app. Replace this with a native SwiftUI sheet triggered from a 'Download Model' button alongside Load Model. The sheet should: (1) list available models fetched from HuggingFace (mrt2_small, mrt2_base) with size/hardware requirements shown, (2) show a download progress bar using URLSession with async/await, (3) save to ~/Documents/Magenta/magenta-rt-v2/models/<name>/, (4) auto-invoke loadModel on the downloaded .mlxfn file on completion. The HF repo is google/magenta-realtime-2, models live under the models/ subdirectory. No Python or mrt CLI dependency — pure Swift URLSession download.

title: Replace simulation timer with real engine audio and metrics
description: Two changes in Managers.swift: (1) AVAudioSourceNode render block — instead of zeroing the buffer, call magentart_read_audio(handle, leftPtr, rightPtr, frameCount) on each render callback. The engine generates stereo 48kHz float32 samples. (2) Remove the simulationTimer. Instead, start a 100ms DispatchSourceTimer on a background queue that calls magentart_get_metrics(handle, &metricsStruct) and dispatches the result to MainActor to update state.metrics and state.audioLevels (compute RMS of the last buffer fill for levels). Stop the timer in stopSimulation() and reset audioLevels to (0,0).

title: Implement PlayerManager.loadModel via C bridge
description: Replace the stub in Managers.swift. PlayerManager needs to own a MagentaEngineRef (opaque handle from magentart_create()). loadModel(at:) should call magentart_load_model(handle, path.cString), check the bool return, and on success update state.modelName to the filename. On failure set self.error to a descriptive Error so the UI shows the errorAlert. Add a deinit that calls magentart_destroy(). Guard togglePlay so it is a no-op (with an error) if no model is loaded yet.

title: Update swift-player/README.md to reflect mrt2-build delegation
description: swift-player/README.md Quick Start and Makefile table still describe build-mrt2 and setup as if they run inside swift-player/. Now that swift-player/Makefile delegates to ../mrt2-build, update: (1) Quick Start to note delegation and preferred root entry point, (2) Makefile table descriptions for setup/build-mrt2, (3) add clean-legacy entry, (4) remove mention of internal magenta-realtime clone.

title: Remove orphaned swift-player/magenta-realtime/ clone after mrt2-build migration
description: When swift-player/Makefile had its own build-mrt2 target it cloned magenta-realtime into swift-player/magenta-realtime/. That directory still exists on disk (git-ignored by swift-player/.gitignore). The new Makefile no longer clones or references it. Add a clean-legacy target to swift-player/Makefile that removes this directory, and optionally add a note to swift-player/README.md pointing developers to run it once.
close_reason: clean-legacy target added to swift-player/Makefile; orphaned 3.0 GB clone removed

title: Update rust-player to link against shared mrt2-build/libmagentart-core.a
description: rust-player/Cargo.toml uses cxx-build which compiles C++ at cargo build time. Add a build.rs that reads MRT2_BUILD_DIR (defaulting to ../mrt2-build) and links against the pre-built libmagentart-core.a and headers in include/magentart/. This avoids each player re-compiling the same C++ code.

title: Update swift-player/Makefile to use shared mrt2-build/ instead of private clone
description: swift-player/Makefile currently clones magenta-realtime into swift-player/magenta-realtime/ and builds it locally. Refactor it to accept MRT2_BUILD_DIR (defaulting to ../mrt2-build) and skip the clone+cmake steps when the shared library already exists. The build-mrt2 target should delegate to the root mrt2-build/Makefile rather than duplicating it.

title: Shared MRT2 Build System
description: Create a common MRT2 build infrastructure shared between Swift and Rust player projects to simplify setup and enable build artifact reuse

title: Replace manual dot-spinner with indicatif for loading/encoding phases
description: Swap the hand-rolled print!(".") polling loop (model load, MusicCoCa asset init, prompt encoding) for proper animated spinners using the indicatif crate. Small, low-risk DX win.

title: Add ratatui live TUI dashboard for real-time metrics
description: Replace the scrolling [Metrics] tick: NNN log lines with an in-place ratatui + crossterm dashboard showing transformer ms, dropped frames, ring buffer fill %, uptime, and current prompt/temperature/top-k/model. Consider tui-realm if Elm-style Model/Update/View composition is preferred over raw ratatui immediate-mode rendering.

title: Add version info display and About menu item to swift-player
description: The app has no version info visible to the user. Two surfaces: (1) About menu item — add CommandGroup(replacing: .appInfo) in MagentaPlayerApp .commands block with a Button('About Magenta Player') that shows an NSAlert or sheet with app name, version string, model name, and a link to the magenta-realtime repo. Version should be read from Info.plist CFBundleShortVersionString (currently 0.1.0) so it stays in sync. (2) Optionally show model version/size in the header subtitle below the model filename — e.g. 'mrt2_small · 230M'. The model size can be inferred from the filename. Update CFBundleShortVersionString in Info.plist to track real releases.

title: Design and add app icon to swift-player .app bundle
description: MagentaPlayer.app currently has no icon (generic document icon in the Dock). Create an AppIcon.appiconset with the required macOS sizes (16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024 pt at 1x and 2x). Icon concept: something that evokes real-time audio generation — waveform, neural/lattice motif, or abstract music visualization. Add the .appiconset to swift-player/Resources/Assets.xcassets. Wire into the .app bundle by copying the compiled .icns file into MagentaPlayer.app/Contents/Resources/ and declaring CFBundleIconFile in Info.plist. The Makefile build-swift and run-swift assembly steps should include the icon copy. Asset generation can use actool (part of Xcode CLI) or iconutil to compile the .appiconset to .icns.

title: Expose blend weights for multi-prompt mixing in swift-player
description: set_blend_weight(i, w) and set_blend_weights(weights, count) allow mixing up to 6 MusicCoCa style embeddings simultaneously, enabling real-time style interpolation. The original Magenta UI uses a 2D XY pad (PromptSurface) for this. In swift-player: (1) Add text fields for prompt slots 0–5 (initially show just 2, expandable). (2) Add weight sliders per slot. (3) Add magentart_set_blend_weight and magentart_set_text_prompts_weighted to bridge (set_text_prompts takes vector<string> + vector<float>). This is the most powerful creative control after the basic prompt. See docs/mrt2-prompt-and-drift.md and realtime_runner.h for set_blend_weight API.

title: Add real-time audio resampler to CPAL callback
description: Implement a lock-free, zero-allocation linear or cubic resampler inside rust-player's CPAL callback to resample 48 kHz MRT2 frames to the hardware default rate (e.g. 44.1 kHz) if 48 kHz is unsupported by the output device

title: Connect MIDIManager note events to C bridge
description: MIDIInputPortCreateWithBlock callback in MIDIManager currently does nothing. Parse incoming MIDIPacketList for note-on (status 0x9x) and note-off (status 0x8x or velocity=0) events. Forward each to the engine via a new magentart_send_midi_note(handle, note, velocity, isOn) C bridge call — or directly call magentart::core::RealtimeRunner::send_midi_note if that is the actual API (check realtime_runner.h once headers are available). MIDIManager needs a reference to the engine handle; pass it in via its init, or publish events via a closure that PlayerManager subscribes to (preferred, keeps MIDIManager decoupled).

title: Add mrt2-build/ configuration and documentation
description: Create documentation and configuration for mrt2-build/ including paths, usage examples, and CI instructions

title: Update root README.md monorepo layout to document mrt2-build/ shared build directory
description: The root README.md does not mention mrt2-build/ in the Monorepo Layout section. Update it to explain that mrt2-build/ is the shared C++ inference engine artifact directory, how to build it once (make build-mrt2 from the root), and that both swift-player and rust-player depend on it.
